title: My Daughter Forged Lease, Locked Me Out, And Stole My $340,000. So I Took Her House At Auction.
description: After my husband died, I sold our $340,000 home to pay off my daughter's debts, only for her to forge a lease and lock me out. I sued for elder exploitation, won a $410,000 judgment, and claimed her six-bedroom house at auction where I now house displaced older women. She still gets a monthly invoice for the property taxes she owes.
thumb_text: SHE FORGED MY LEASE I TOOK HER HOUSE
pinned_comment: If your daughter forged a lease, locked you out after you sold your home for $340,000 to pay her debts, and you later ended up owning her house at auction, what would you have done? Share your story in the comments!

thumb_lines.text: SOLD MY HOME TO COVER HER DEBTS, THEN LOCKED ME OUT
